steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The researchers hypothesize that PCFG whales' smaller size is 'an adaptation to distinct resource opportunities' in their foraging range. To directly support this, a finding must show both (1) what those resources are and (2) why smaller size would be advantageous for accessing them.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'closer proximity to major ports and urban populations': irrelevant to foraging resources or body size — ✗
  • Choice B — 'average body size has remained relatively steady': just confirms the size pattern exists without explaining any adaptive advantage — ✗
  • Choice C — 'forage in rocky kelp beds at shallow depths inaccessible to whales as large as those in the ENP main group': directly shows that the PCFG foraging habitat requires smaller body size to access — ✓
  • Choice D — 'certain crustacean prey species... are not available in Arctic waters': identifies different prey but does not explain why being smaller would be necessary to access it — ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
